prompts.abstract: A cylinder with a rectangular top and a cylindrical base. The top has a flat surface with rounded edges.
prompts.description: A cylinder with a rectangular top and a cylindrical base. The top has a flat surface with rounded edges.
prompts.beginner: Design a rounded rectangular lid with a compact shape. The lid measures about three-quarters of an inch in length, half an inch in width, and just over a quarter of an inch in height. This lid fits perfectly over a container, providing a secure and sleek cover.
prompts.intermediate: Start by setting up a new coordinate system with a rotation and translation that aligns the work plane correctly. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face, forming a loop with four lines that create a unique shape. After completing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired dimensions. Transform this scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by applying the same rotation and translation used in the coordinate system. Extrude the sketch to create the solid part, ensuring it has the correct height. The final rounded rectangular lid has a length of zero point seven five, a width of zero point five, and a height of zero point zero seven five eight. This part will serve as a lid and can be assembled with other components to form a complete design.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero, zero point zero seven five eight, and zero. On the first face, draw a two-dimensional sketch consisting of a loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the coordinates zero, zero point five and ends at zero point five, zero. The second line extends from zero point five, zero to zero point seven five, zero. The third line runs from zero point seven five, zero to zero point two five, zero point five. Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by connecting from zero point two five, zero point five back to zero, zero point five. After drawing the sketch, apply a scale factor of zero point seven five to the entire sketch to reduce its size proportionally. Next, transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional object by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it with the same vector used in the coordinate system setup. To generate the three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch zero point zero seven five eight units in the direction of the normal, ensuring that no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Perform a boolean operation to create a new solid body from this part. The dimensions of the rounded rectangular lid are zero point seven five in length, zero point five in width, and zero point zero seven five eight in height.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero, zero point zero seven five eight, and zero. On the first face, draw a two-dimensional sketch consisting of a loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the coordinates zero, zero point five and ends at zero point five, zero. The second line extends from zero point five, zero to zero point seven five, zero. The third line runs from zero point seven five, zero to zero point two five, zero point five. Finally, the fourth line completes the loop by connecting from zero point two five, zero point five back to zero, zero point five. After drawing the sketch, apply a scale factor of zero point seven five to the entire sketch to reduce its size proportionally. Next, transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into a three-dimensional object by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it with the same vector used in the coordinate system setup. To generate the three-dimensional model, extrude the sketch zero point zero seven five eight units in the direction of the normal, ensuring that no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Perform a boolean operation to create a new solid body from this part. The dimensions of the rounded rectangular lid are zero point seven five in length, zero point five in width, and zero point zero seven five eight in height.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by setting up a new coordinate system with a rotation and translation that aligns the work plane correctly. Draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face, forming a loop with four lines that create a unique shape. After completing the sketch, scale it down to fit the desired dimensions. Transform this scaled sketch into a three-dimensional object by applying the same rotation and translation used in the coordinate system. Extrude the sketch to create the solid part, ensuring it has the correct height. The final rounded rectangular lid has a length of zero point seven five, a width of zero point five, and a height of zero point zero seven five eight. This part will serve as a lid and can be assembled with other components to form a complete design.
</level2>

<level3>
Design a rounded rectangular lid with a compact shape. The lid measures about three-quarters of an inch in length, half an inch in width, and just over a quarter of an inch in height. This lid fits perfectly over a container, providing a secure and sleek cover.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rounded Rectangular Lid

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.0758, 0.0].

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Line 1:** Draw a line from (0.0, 0.5) to (0.5, 0.0).
    - **Line 2:** Draw a line from (0.5, 0.0) to (0.75, 0.0).
    - **Line 3:** Draw a line from (0.75, 0.0) to (0.25, 0.5).
    - **Line 4:** Draw a line from (0.25, 0.5) to (0.0, 0.5).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scale factor of 0.75 to the entire sketch.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.0758, 0.0].

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch 0.0758 units towards the normal.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.

#### Boolean Operation
- Create a new solid body from this part.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.75
- Width: 0.5
- Height: 0.0758

</part_1>
